title: Pass 5.A.2: ferrotorch-mps MpsDevice + 8 op STUBs (Apple-silicon inference blocker)
description:
Pass 4 finding (umbrella #982) — ferrotorch-mps audit, STUB bucket (8 findings).

Cluster of stubs that make the MPS backend unusable end-to-end:
- `MpsDevice::new` always returns Err
- `mps_device_count` always returns 0
- `neg_f32`, `gelu_f32`, `dropout_f32`, ... 8 op-level stubs

Net effect: no Apple-silicon user can construct an MPS device; even if they could, hot ops silently err. All 8 stubs are reachable from public API.

Acceptance:
- Wire MpsDevice::new + device_count to real Metal device discovery, OR
- Mark crate `#[deprecated]` and remove from default features with crosslink follow-up
- Implement or honestly Err on the 8 stub op kernels with discriminating fixtures.
